title: Selling Ebooks Without Stripe: Which Option Actually Works for You
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: If you're a digital creator in a country where PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, or Payhip are restricted, you're not alone. Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Ethiopia, Venezuela, Iran, Cameroon, Tanzania, and the Philippines are just a few of the places where mainstream payment processors have limited or no support. As a result, selling ebooks online becomes a challenging and frustrating process. This post is for those creators who have been blocked, excluded, or simply can't access the tools they need to get paid for their work.
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip are well-known platforms for selling digital products, but they come with their own set of limitations. PayPal, for instance, is blocked in many countries, including some mentioned above, and requires a business account to receive payments. Stripe is only available in select countries and has a complex fee structure. Gumroad and Payhip have their own restrictions and often require a subscription or commission fees. In contrast, Unchained Commerce offers a complete store infrastructure with the Full Stack Bundle, which includes the Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, all for a one-time payment of $79.99. This solution provides instant automated delivery, global reach, and no subscription or commission fees.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

The payment processors mentioned above often exclude certain countries due to regulatory requirements, limited banking support, or high risk profiles. Unchained Commerce, on the other hand, has developed a solution that works around these restrictions. With NOWPayments as the payment processor, creators can accept 300+ coins without requiring a KYC (Know Your Customer) process or a buyer account. This means that creators in restricted countries can finally sell their ebooks without the need for a PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, or Payhip account.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

When it comes to payment speed, fees, and real costs, Unchained Commerce stands out from the competition. With the Full Stack Bundle, creators can enjoy instant automated delivery, live price feeds, and a fee structure that's transparent and predictable. In contrast, PayPal and Stripe often charge high fees, sometimes reaching up to 3.4% per transaction. Gumroad and Payhip also have their own fee structures, which can be confusing and costly for creators.
Unchained Commerce offers a complete store infrastructure that works around PayPal and Stripe restrictions.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

The risk of platform dependency is asymmetric in a specific way that most creators underestimate. When PayPal or Stripe is working, everything seems fine. When it stops working — through suspension, policy change, or geographic restriction enforcement — you lose access to your revenue channel instantly, often with funds already inside the platform that you cannot withdraw. Rebuilding on a new platform takes weeks. The income gap during that period is real money lost. Crypto-native infrastructure removes this asymmetric risk. Your wallet cannot be suspended. Your GitHub Pages store cannot be taken down by a payment platform's compliance team. The infrastructure is under your control from day one, which means no single external decision can cut off your income.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

Ultimately, the choice between Unchained Commerce and the mainstream payment processors depends on your specific situation. If you're a digital creator in a restricted country, Unchained Commerce offers a complete store infrastructure that works around these limitations. If you're looking for a more traditional solution with a subscription or commission fees, then PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, or Payhip might be a better fit. With the Full Stack Bundle, creators can get started with a one-time payment of $79.99 and enjoy instant automated delivery, global reach, and no subscription or commission fees.
